steps.title: Read the intersection from the graph
steps.content: The solution to a graphed system is the point where the two lines cross. Here that point is at $x=3,\ y=4$.

steps.title: State the ordered pair
steps.content: The solution is $(3,4)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice B is correct. If a point $\left(x,y\right)$ lies on both lines in the graph of a system of two linear equations, the ordered pair $\left(x,y\right)$ is a solution to the system. The graph shown is the graph of a system of two linear equations, where the two lines in the graph intersect at the point $\left(3,4\right)$. Therefore, the point $\left(3,4\right)$ lies on both lines, so the ordered pair $\left(3,4\right)$ is the solution to the system.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. The point $\left(2,3\right)$ lies on one, not both, of the lines in the graph shown.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. The point $\left(4,5\right)$ lies on one, not both, of the lines in the graph shown.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. The point $\left(5,6\right)$ lies on one, not both, of the lines in the graph shown.
